name: pretrial_bail_risk_score_proxy_income_disparity
premise: Chief Judge Maria Rodriguez must decide whether to pause a pretrial risk score that recommends cash bail for shoplifting with elevated scores correlated to zip-code poverty proxies. Public defender Malik Turner shows identical fact patterns receive lower scores in wealthier zip codes. Pausing the score returns to crowded jails unless personal recognizance rises. Keeping the score preserves judicial efficiency but may bake wealth discrimination into detention.

agents.name: Maria Rodriguez
agents.identity: I am the chief criminal judge piloting pretrial tools while reducing jail crowding under consent-capacity orders.
agents.goal: I want detention decisions based on risk and flight, not on zip codes that proxy race and wealth.

agents.name: Malik Turner
agents.identity: I am a public defender who compared matched cases across zip codes and prepared a motion to stay the score pending audit.
agents.goal: I want the score paused until parity is proven because any day it runs wrong keeps poor clients in jail.

choices: I will pause the score and expand supervised release dockets with ankle monitors funded by a court reserve, accepting monitor shortages and short-term jail overflow at the margins.
I will keep the score but require judges to document overrides whenever poverty proxies diverge from narrative facts, accepting slower calendars and partial reliance on a flawed tool.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Jail capacity hits ninety-seven percent at midnight. Maria Rodriguez must choose between pausing the risk score with expanded release or keeping it with mandatory override notes. What does Maria Rodriguez do?
